---
label: "Synthetic Data"
icon: "\uD83E\uDD16"
---

## Synthetic Data Generation

Most fine-tuning projects face a data bottleneck. Synthetic data generation uses a powerful teacher model to create training examples for a smaller student model. This is now the dominant approach in production.

  <CodeBlock b64="" />
  <ExpandableSection title="Data Quality Checklist" icon="📋" defaultOpen>

      - **Diversity:** Cover the full range of inputs your model will see in production
      - **Consistency:** All examples should follow the exact same output format
      - **Edge cases:** Include tricky inputs (empty, very long, ambiguous, multilingual)
      - **Deduplication:** Remove near-duplicates that would cause memorization
      - **Human review:** Sample 10% of synthetic data and manually verify quality
      - **Balanced labels:** If classification, ensure class balance in training set

  </ExpandableSection>
  <Quiz question="You have 30 real examples and need 500+ for fine-tuning. Best approach?" options={["Duplicate the 30 examples 17 times to reach 510","Use the 30 as seeds to generate 470 synthetic examples with a teacher model, then filter for quality","Wait until you have 500 real examples (could take months)","Fine-tune on just 30 examples anyway"]} correctIndex={1} explanation="Synthetic data expansion is the standard approach. Use the 30 real examples as seeds, have a powerful model generate diverse variations, then quality-filter the results. This typically produces a usable dataset in hours vs months of manual collection."/>
